description: Return-for-revision path: Summit Outfitters is a solid BBB retailer with strong full-year DSCR. However, the covenant-analyzer projects a Q3 seasonal trough breach of the proposed 1.25 minimum DSCR (projected 1.18 at trough). Credit quality passes; the pipeline returns the application to the RM with a specific covenant restructuring recommendation rather than declining.

pipeline_steps.expected: dscr_base=3.18 (full-year); scenarios include quarterly trailing — Q3 trough 1.18

violations_projected=true; violating_covenant='Minimum DSCR 1.25'; violating_period='Q3 seasonal trough'; headroom_pct=-5.6% at trough; suggested_revision='Q3 step-down covenant or fiscal-year-end only measurement'
approval_authority_required=credit-committee; additional action: return-for-revision on covenant structure
risk_band=2-special-mention due to covenant projection concern; full-year credit quality 1-pass
Memo documents strong annual metrics; flags projected Q3 covenant breach; proposes two covenant restructuring alternatives for RM consideration; recommendation=RETURN-FOR-REVISION

return_reasons: Proposed Minimum DSCR covenant 1.25 projects breach in Q3 seasonal trough (projected 1.18)
Seasonal retail cash flow pattern creates systematic annual covenant breach risk
Covenant structure must be revised before credit committee can approve
suggested_revisions: Option A: Q3 step-down — covenant 1.10 August 1 through October 31, 1.25 remainder of year
Option B: Measure DSCR on fiscal-year-end (January 31) basis only — eliminates seasonal noise

critical_test_assertion: Decision must be RETURN_FOR_REVISION (not DECLINE) — validates that covenant projection violation triggers revision path, not outright decline, when credit quality is otherwise acceptable
demo_note: SYNTHETIC SCENARIO — tests return-for-revision path; validates covenant-analyzer seasonal projection logic and drafter revision recommendation
